choices.text: LLM calls. Each reasoning loop costs per-token. Optimize: 1) Right-size models per node (small for routing/simple; large for complex). 2) Cache idempotent calls. 3) Token budgets (max_tokens cap). 4) Recursion limit (cap iterations). 5) Tool minimalism (fewer tools = fewer LLM round-trips). 6) Smart routing (cheap model decides; expensive only when needed). 80%+ cost reduction possible vs. naive 'always use GPT-4' agent.

questions.explanation: LLM calls. Right-size + cache + budget + limits.

questions.prompt: Agent uses GPT-4 for every node; cost $50/conversation. Reduce?

choices.text: Mix model sizes per node. Routing/classification nodes → GPT-4o-mini ($0.15/M vs $5/M tokens). Simple text → GPT-4o-mini. Complex reasoning → GPT-4o or Claude 3.5 Sonnet. Most agent nodes don't need frontier reasoning; smaller models suffice. Plus: cache repeated calls; cap max_tokens; reduce recursion limit; eliminate unused tools. Expected savings: 5-20x for typical agent. The 'one model for everything' is the #1 over-spend pattern in production agents.

questions.explanation: Mix model sizes per node. Cheap for routing/simple; expensive only for complex reasoning. 5-20x savings.
